differential_diagnoses: Primary cardiac diffuse large B-cell lymphoma
Primary pulmonary squamous cell carcinoma with direct intracardiac invasion
next_steps: 18F-FDG PET-CT scan to identify potential primary malignancy sites and metabolic activity
Endobronchial ultrasound-guided transbronchial needle aspiration (EBUS-TBNA) of mediastinal lymph nodes for definitive tissue diagnosis
Cardiac MRI with contrast to evaluate the degree of myocardial infiltration and vascularity of the mass
Serum lactate dehydrogenase (LDH) level and serum beta-2 microglobulin for lymphoma workup
CT-guided biopsy of the pulmonary nodules if nodal sampling is non-diagnostic
Continued systemic anticoagulation for management of segmental pulmonary emboli
